summary: Audience recording captures a spirited early‑1970 show with standout performances of “He Was A Friend Of Mine”, “Walking The Dog” and the Viola Lee Blues → The Seven combo, while sound quality ranges from lively to muddy.
review: The concert is praised for its rare set – early renditions of “Friend Of The Devil”, a haunting “He Was A Friend Of Mine”, and an eclectic mix that includes “Walking The Dog” and the Viola Lee Blues → The Seven sequence.  Although the audience tape is generally listenable and even described as “stereo‑like”, several reviewers note channel wobble, bass‑heavy mix and occasional tape‑speed issues.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers give high marks for the performance and highlight many classic moments; recording flaws are noted but do not dominate, resulting in an overall strong rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Tape is lively with a pleasant ambience, but suffers from right‑channel wobble, bass‑heavy mix, muddy vocals and occasional tape‑curl/speed issues that require EQ and slight pitch correction.

standout_songs: He Was A Friend Of Mine
show_quality.setlist_flow: A single early‑show set that weaves folk‑rock cuts with rare blues numbers; highlights include the Viola Lee Blues → The Seven transition, a spirited “Me & My Uncle”, and crowd‑driven moments like the Dark Star call‑out.  The flow is energetic but occasionally uneven due to the band adjusting to new material.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s tone on “He Was A Friend Of Mine” is praised, yet some note he sounds “all over the place” and struggles with NYC crowd restraint.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s bass leads into a compelling Cumberland jam during The Seven sequence.
